name: Willow Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Willow. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Willow surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength optimized for Willow absorption and minimal penetration

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning with adequate energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage and thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with controlled thermal effects

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient area coverage and effective cleaning

passCount.description: Optimal number of passes for complete cleaning without excessive processing

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning coverage

dwellTime.description: Optimal dwell time per spot for effective cleaning action

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm fiber lasers in wood cleaning to balance efficiency and avoid charring; starting at 150 W for willow's low thermal conductivity (~0.15 W/mK) prevents substrate damage. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs and studies on laser wood surface treatment (e.g., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contaminant removal on porous woods like willow; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal balance. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and wood cleaning case studies (Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for 1064 nm cleaning of organics like willow to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ <50 μm); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precision but costlier. 50 ns recommended for willow's reflectivity (~5-10% at 1064 nm). Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning to ensure coverage without excessive heat buildup in low-melting-point woods (willow ~250-300°C char point); higher rates speed up process but risk accumulation. 50 kHz balances speed and safety. Source: Academic literature on wood ablation (Journal of Applied Physics, 2017).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal without ablating willow substrate; damage threshold ~5-10 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to high absorption. Recommended 2 J/cm² for safe cleaning. Conservative estimate for uncertainty in willow-specific data. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Optics Express (2021).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise cleaning on wood grains; smaller for fine details (100-200 μm), larger for broad areas (300-500 μm). 200 μm recommended for uniform willow surface prep. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes typical for complete removal on willow without overexposure. Source: Industry practice in heritage wood restoration case studies (Studies in Conservation, 2016).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular wood surfaces like willow; 30-50% for speed, 60-80% for thoroughness. 60% recommended to avoid gaps. Source: Process optimization in laser scanning studies (Procedia Manufacturing, 2019).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation Thresholds of Wood Species for Cultural Heritage Conservation
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Bella et al., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2019, DOI: 10.1016/j.culher.2019.03.005
laserDamageThreshold.context: Dry willow wood (Salix alba, density 0.40 g/cm³, moisture content <5%), 25°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser (10 ns pulse), threshold defined as onset of visible ablation via optical microscopy

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation Thresholds of Different Wood Species for Femtosecond Pulses
ablationThreshold.citation: Hossain, M. et al., Applied Physics A: Materials Science & Processing, 2018, DOI: 10.1007/s00339-018-1675-4
ablationThreshold.context: Natural willow wood (Salix alba, density 0.40 g/cm³), room temperature (20°C), 800 nm femtosecond laser (pulse duration 150 fs), measured under vacuum conditions
